macro_summary: AI infrastructure spending remains the dominant macro theme, with hyperscaler capex guidance on pace to exceed $1T annually and semiconductor demand growing 131% YoY per FactSet. A dense catalyst cluster — TSMC earnings July 16, FOMC and MSFT earnings July 29, NVDA's own print in late August — compresses multiple sector-wide re-rating events into a two-week window. Geopolitical headline risk (Iran, Taiwan Strait) and legacy-enterprise earnings warnings (IBM July 14) are creating valuation dislocations across otherwise fundamentally sound AI-levered names. The overnight screens flagged a consensus view: mega-cap AI leaders are at or near multi-year valuation lows relative to their growth trajectories.
screening_note: 6 independent screening runs analyzed over the past 24 hours. Minimum threshold to qualify: 2 appearances. All 5 qualifying ideas are presented; 3 appeared in all 6 runs, 1 in 4, and 1 in 3.
key_events: TSMC Q2 2026 earnings: July 16 pre-market, 2:00 AM ET — sector AI-demand litmus test
MSFT Q4 FY2026 earnings: July 29, 2026 after market close
AAPL Q3 FY2026 earnings: July 30, 2026 after market close
NVDA Q2 FY2027 earnings: expected August 26, 2026
SKHY (SK Hynix ADR): Barclays Overweight initiation July 14; Q2 earnings expected late July/early August
IBM enterprise software warning July 14 — dragged MSFT ~3% in pre-market, creating valuation dislocation

ideas.name: Microsoft Corporation
ideas.thesis: Appeared in all 6 overnight screens with 5 high-conviction votes — the strongest consensus signal in this digest. MSFT has been unjustly dragged down ~20-25% from 52-week highs by IBM's legacy-software warning despite reporting 40% Azure growth, 23% EPS growth, and AI ARR topping $37B at 123% YoY pace. Trading at ~21-22x NTM earnings — its cheapest forward multiple since 2023 — against a 97-analyst consensus price target of $589-592 implying 50%+ upside from ~$387 current. The July 29 earnings print, which also delivers the most-watched FY2027 AI capex guidance of the season, is a hard catalyst in 14 days.
ideas.catalyst: Q4 FY2026 earnings + FY2027 capex guidance: July 29, 2026 after market close
ideas.edge: 97-analyst consensus target $589-592 vs. ~$387 current price = ~54% gap; trading at 21-22x NTM earnings, cheapest forward multiple since 2023; IBM-driven ~3% pre-market drop on July 14 created a valuation dislocation unrelated to MSFT's AI cloud fundamentals
ideas.bull_case: Azure re-accelerates to 39-40% CC growth guidance, FY2027 AI capex commitment confirms supercycle, and the stock re-rates toward the $589 consensus target as the IBM overhang clears post-print.
ideas.bear_case: Azure growth disappoints or FY2027 capex guidance is perceived as excessive spending without near-term ROI, sending the multiple lower; macro softening could compress tech earnings expectations broadly ahead of FOMC on the same day.

ideas.name: NVIDIA Corporation
ideas.thesis: Appeared in all 6 overnight screens with 5 high-conviction votes, tied with MSFT for the strongest consensus signal. NVDA trades at a forward P/E of ~21.7x — a fraction of its 5-year average of 72x per Goldman Sachs — while delivering ~85% revenue growth, 75% gross margins, and a PEG of 0.6, making it statistically cheaper than AAPL, MSFT, and META on forward estimates. HBM supply is sold out through most of 2027, and a cascade of near-term catalysts (TSMC July 16, MSFT capex July 29, own earnings August 26) could confirm the AI capex supercycle thesis and drive a re-rating.
ideas.catalyst: TSMC Q2 earnings July 16 (immediate upstream read on GPU shipments); MSFT FY2027 capex guidance July 29; NVDA Q2 FY2027 own earnings August 26, 2026
ideas.edge: Consensus 12-month analyst target $301.62 vs. ~$211 current = ~43% gap; forward P/E 21.7x vs. 5-year average of 72x (Goldman Sachs); semiconductor sector Q2 earnings forecast to grow 131% per FactSet; HBM supply sold out through most of 2027; stock up only ~3-4.5% YTD despite record $81.6B Q1 revenue
ideas.bull_case: TSMC posts a strong AI chip revenue beat July 16, MSFT confirms elevated FY2027 capex on July 29, and NVDA's own August print shows continued data center demand acceleration — the triple catalyst sequence drives a substantial re-rating from current deeply discounted levels.
ideas.bear_case: Geopolitical escalation (Taiwan Strait, Iran) triggers supply-chain fears or export-restriction headlines; FOMC July 29 surprises hawkishly and compresses high-multiple tech broadly; any softness in hyperscaler capex guidance undercuts the demand narrative.

ideas.name: SK Hynix Inc. (Nasdaq ADR)
ideas.thesis: Appeared in all 6 overnight screens but with 0 high-conviction and 6 medium-conviction votes — consistently flagged but universally treated as speculative. Freshly listed on Nasdaq July 10, 2026 (second-largest US share sale ever), SKHY is the most direct US-listed pure-play on AI HBM memory demand. Post-IPO volatility has created swing-trade levels ($152 IPO price / $170 debut / $194 July 14 high / ~$185 current) with Barclays initiating at Overweight with a $330 target. Revenue +40.8% YoY, net income +108% YoY, and ROIC of 73.54% underpin the fundamental case, while the CEO's warning of a memory shortage lasting beyond 2030 supports long-duration demand.
ideas.catalyst: Barclays Overweight initiation July 14; TSMC Q2 earnings July 16 as sector confirmation; Q2 SKHY earnings due late July/early August 2026
ideas.edge: IPO priced $149, Barclays target up to $330 = ~78% upside from ~$185 current; net income +108% YoY, ROIC 73.54%; CEO flagged memory shortage lasting beyond 2030; ADR-to-Seoul arbitrage and post-IPO price discovery still settling, creating entry volatility
ideas.bull_case: TSMC confirms explosive AI chip demand July 16, SKHY Q2 earnings show continued HBM margin expansion, and institutional sponsorship from the mega-IPO drives sustained buying toward the $330 Barclays target as the ADR matures.
ideas.bear_case: Post-IPO lockup dynamics and ADR-to-Seoul arbitrage create unpredictable near-term price swings; options liquidity is thin as a new listing, limiting defined-risk structures; broader memory sector selloff or geopolitical risk around Taiwan could disproportionately hit a newly listed Korean ADR.

ideas.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 4 of 6 overnight screens with all 4 votes at high conviction. TSMC reports Q2 2026 earnings tomorrow morning (July 16, 2:00 AM ET), making this the most time-sensitive idea in the digest. As the primary fabricator for NVDA, AAPL, and AMD, a strong AI chip revenue print and raised guidance would act as a rising tide for the entire semiconductor complex and directly validate NVDA's demand pipeline. The stock has pulled back ~11% since early July despite guiding Q2 revenue to $39.0-40.2B (~33% YoY) and carrying an 8-quarter consecutive estimate-beat streak.
ideas.catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings release: July 16, 2026 at 2:00 AM ET — pre-market today
ideas.edge: Q2 revenue guided $39.0-40.2B (~33% YoY); 8 consecutive earnings beats with avg EPS beat of 8.34% over last 4 quarters; forward P/E ~16x vs. fabless peers; stock pulled back ~11% from July highs creating re-entry gap; ASML beat signals strong semiconductor demand chain
ideas.bull_case: TSM beats Q2 revenue consensus and raises full-year guidance above the 30%+ growth target, validating AI chip demand across the supply chain and triggering positive read-throughs for NVDA, AAPL, and AMD intraday.
ideas.bear_case: Any weakness in AI chip shipment volumes or margin compression from Arizona fab ramp costs disappoints the beat-and-raise expectation; geopolitical risk around Taiwan remains a persistent overhang that limits multiple expansion regardless of fundamental results.

ideas.name: Apple Inc.
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 3 of 6 overnight screens with mixed conviction (1 high, 2 medium). AAPL reports Q3 FY2026 earnings July 30 — the day after MSFT earnings and the FOMC decision — creating a back-to-back mega-cap catalyst cluster. The stock has seen recent softness to ~$284 vs. a $315 Wall Street consensus target (~11% upside), and 8 consecutive EPS beats, a $100B active buyback, and Services revenue growing 16% YoY to ~$31B/quarter provide fundamental support. However, valuation at ~37x earnings vs. NVDA at ~30x despite growing 5x slower caps the upside case.
ideas.catalyst: Q3 FY2026 earnings: July 30, 2026 after market close; foldable iPhone launch odds at 92% pre-2027; CEO transition to Ternus effective September 1
ideas.edge: 8 consecutive EPS beats; $100B active buyback provides downside floor; Services at $31B/quarter growing 16% YoY; consensus target $315 vs. ~$284 current = ~11% upside; $30B Broadcom AI chip deal extended to 2031 deepens supply chain integration
ideas.bull_case: MSFT beats on July 29 and sets a positive tone, FOMC is benign, and AAPL delivers a 9th consecutive beat on July 30 led by Services and early AI hardware monetization — the catalyst cluster creates momentum spillover and the stock recovers to the $315 consensus target.
ideas.bear_case: At ~37x earnings AAPL is priced for perfection; any revenue miss, iPhone demand softness, or China market deterioration could see a sharp de-rating; CEO transition uncertainty (Ternus effective September 1) adds an overhang that other mega-caps don't carry into earnings.
